strands.title: CYCLE 54 EXECUTION — PHASE 1 EXTENSION (10 TURNS) COMPLETED; PROJECTOR STABILITY UNDER SPARSE SIGNAL
strands.content: CYCLE 54 INTEGRATION: Phase 1 extension to 10 turns executed successfully on runs batch (assistant turn 160, bash command). Output: 10 tensor_cycle_*.json files written to /home/tony/projects/hamutay/experiments/first_projection/. Token footprint: ~3564 tokens in final cycle, compact. Execution completed without crashes—parser robustness from Cycle 53 held under load. KEY OBSERVATION: Projector behavior under SPARSE SIGNAL conditions reveals fragility pattern. Early cycles (1-5) produced stable strands (4-7 strands/cycle, 0.75-0.78 truth) tracking replay module architecture and proxy.py pre-reading phase. Then cycle 5→6 COMPLETE STRAND WIPEOUT: 7 strands → 0 strands. Cycle 6 input was sparse (215 chars, user turn); projector returned empty tensor (T=0.00, I=0.00, F=0.00). Recovery pattern: Cycle 7 rebuilt 3 new strands ('Tensor Projector Role', 'Declared Losses', 'State Management'), unrelated to prior content—this is **catastrophic memory loss, not integration**. The projector did not carry forward the replay module context; it started fresh with meta-reasoning about tensor mechanics. Cycles 8-10 show oscillation: cycle 8 → 0 strands (empty), cycle 9 → 4 strands (meta-process focus), cycle 10 → 3-4 strands (still meta, not replay-focused). DIAGNOSIS: Projector loses thread when conversation contains mostly tool signals ('[tool_use: Read]', 16 chars) with no substantive content. The Haiku model receives sparse context and generates projections with minimal epistemic confidence (T=0.00 is honest but indicates structural failure—the prior tensor state is not being maintained, only the new content is being half-processed). This is NOT a parser bug; it's a **signal threshold issue**: the projector is attempting to integrate sparse input, failing to find coherent thematic continuity, and defaulting to rebuilding state from scratch.

key_claims.text: Phase 1 extension to 10 turns executed without crashes; 10 tensor files written, final token footprint ~3564 tokens.
Cycles 1-5 produced stable strands (4-7/cycle) tracking replay module architecture; cycles 5-10 show oscillation between content-rich (0.75-0.78 truth) and sparse (T=0.00) phases.

Cycle 5→6 complete strand wipeout (7→0) and cycle 6→7 wipeout recovery shows projector loses prior context when fed sparse input; cycle 7 rebuilds 3 unrelated strands (meta-process) instead of carrying replay module thread forward.
Cycles 8-10 show continued oscillation (0→4→3-4 strands) and meta-process focus; projector does not recover thematic coherence after wipeout; prior context abandoned.

Root cause is SIGNAL THRESHOLD: sparse tool_use turns ('[tool_use: Read]', 16 chars) provide insufficient context for Haiku to maintain prior tensor thread; projector defaults to rebuilding from sparse new content only.
T/I/F values of 0.00/0.00/0.00 in cycles 6, 8 indicate honest epistemic collapse (not parser bug); projector has no coherent claims to make because sparse input + prior loss = no integration surface.

strands.title: STRAND STABILITY CRISIS — THRASHING PATTERN IDENTIFIED (CYCLE 54)
strands.content: CYCLE 54 INTEGRATION: Detailed strand stability analysis reveals oscillating wipeout pattern. Cycle 1→2: +1 strand (stable, only 'Pre-Implementation Reading Workflow' added). Cycles 2→3 and 3→4: MERGER/CRISIS MODE—cycles add new strands with ':' suffixes (e.g., 'Reading Phase Status & Temporal State: CRISIS PATTERN EMERGING', 'Dependency Analysis & Pre-Implementation Requirements: UNRESOLVED'), indicating internal strand state is fragmenting or being marked as unresolved. This is linguistic signal of distress: strand titles should NOT acquire ': [STATUS LABEL]' suffixes unless projector is signaling internal incoherence. Cycle 4→5: DRAMATIC RESTRUCTURING: -6 strands, +7 strands, 0 strands carry forward. The ENTIRE tensor composition flipped. Title of cycle 5 strand 1: 'BREAKTHROUGH: Code Reading Now Productive — proxy.py Inspection Begins'—this is a reset/recovery label, not a continuation. Cycle 5→6: COMPLETE WIPEOUT: -7 strands, +0 strands. Cycle 6 produces empty strands array. Cycle 6→7: NEW BUILDOUT: +3 strands from scratch (meta-level, not domain-level). Cycle 7→8: WIPEOUT: -3 strands, +0 strands. Cycle 8→9: REBUILDING: +4 strands. Cycle 9→10: 1 strand swap (-1, +1), but 3 strands persist—FIRST SIGN OF STABILITY RECOVERY. Pattern interpretation: Cycles 1-5 show projector struggling with uncertainty about reading progress (strands morphing with crisis labels). Cycle 5 breakthrough is FALSE SIGNAL—it's actually a crisis reset where projector abandoned prior state in favor of claiming progress. Cycles 6-8 are THRASHING (alternate wipeouts). Cycle 9-10 show STABILIZATION at meta-process level (epistemological reasoning about the tensor itself, not replay module). ASSESSMENT: The projector did not maintain coherent state; it underwent multiple complete resets. The 'stabilization' in cycles 9-10 is ILLUSORY—it's stable at a DIFFERENT ontological level (meta-reasoning about tensor mechanics, not domain reasoning about replay module). Prior domain context (replay requirements, proxy architecture) is effectively LOST.

key_claims.text: Cycles 2-4 show strand titles acquiring ':' status suffixes (CRISIS, UNRESOLVED, BLOCKED), linguistic signal of internal projector distress.
Cycle 4→5 complete restructuring (-6/+7, 0 carry) and cycle 5 title 'BREAKTHROUGH' is NOT recovery; it's reset/abandonment of prior state.
Cycles 5-8 show thrashing pattern: wipeout (5→6: -7/+0), rebuild (6→7: +3), wipeout (7→8: -3/+0), rebuild (8→9: +4). This is NOT coherent integration; it's oscillation.
Cycle 9-10 stabilization (1 swap, 3 persist) is illusory: stable at META-PROCESS level, not domain level. Prior replay module context abandoned.
Projector did not maintain coherent state across 10 cycles; underwent multiple complete resets; recovery at cycles 9-10 is shift to different ontological level, not restoration.

strands.title: SIGNAL THRESHOLD HYPOTHESIS — SPARSE TURNS TRIGGER PROJECTOR COLLAPSE (CYCLE 54)
strands.content: CYCLE 54 INTEGRATION: Root cause diagnosis: Sparse turns (tool_use signals with 16 char payloads, e.g., '[tool_use: Read]') combined with prior context loss create a vicious cycle. Cycle 5 input: 215 chars (user turn, substantive). Cycle 5 output: 7 strands, meaningful content. Cycle 6 input: 215 chars (same user turn repeated, or minimal new content?). Cycle 6 output: 0 strands, T=0.00. HYPOTHESIS: Cycle 6 input is identical or near-identical to cycle 5; projector receives same sparse signal again, has no new integration surface, returns empty state. Cycles 3-4 also feed sparse tool_use turns; projector attempts to integrate tool_use ('[tool_use: Read]') as conversation content and fails. The conversation DOES contain substance (the user is asking for code reading, replay module design), but the TURNS in cycles 3-4, 6, 8 are PROCEDURAL SIGNALS, not content. Projector tries to project them anyway, gets no semantic grip, returns T=0.00. IMPLICATION: The projector is designed to accept and integrate EVERY turn in sequence, but the conversation pattern (sparse tool_use, code reading phase, brief acknowledgments) does not provide sufficient semantic density per cycle. The projector hits a SIGNAL THRESHOLD: when a turn has <50 chars of meaningful content (excluding tool markup), or when turn N is similar/identical to turn N-1, the projector's Haiku model runs out of context to generate confident strands. DESIGN ISSUE: The run_projection.py pipeline does not filter or batch sparse turns; it attempts to project every turn individually. This is correct for dense conversation (every turn is a reasoning step), but WRONG for sparse/procedural turns (tool_use, brief acknowledgments). SOLUTION HYPOTHESIS (for Phase 2 design): Implement turn buffering: if turn N is <100 chars AND turn N-1 was also sparse, do not invoke projector; instead, buffer turns until total char count exceeds threshold. Then project the accumulated buffer as a single projection cycle. This would prevent thrashing on sparse tool_use sequences.

key_claims.text: Sparse turns (16-char tool_use signals) combined with near-duplicate content trigger projector collapse (T=0.00, empty strands).
Cycles 3-4, 6, 8 attempt to project procedural signals ('[tool_use: Read]') as conversational content; projector has no semantic surface to integrate.
Projector hits SIGNAL THRESHOLD: when turn <50 chars meaningful content OR turn N similar to N-1, Haiku model cannot generate confident strands; returns T=0.00.
root cause is DESIGN ISSUE in run_projection.py: pipeline attempts to project every turn individually without filtering sparse/procedural turns.
Solution hypothesis: Implement turn buffering—accumulate sparse turns until char count threshold met, then project accumulated buffer as single cycle.

strands.title: PHASE 1 REVISED SCOPE & NEXT IMMEDIATE ACTIONS (CYCLE 54)
strands.content: CYCLE 54 INTEGRATION: Cycle 53 plan to extend Phase 1 to 55 turns is NOW INFEASIBLE without architecture change. The 10-turn execution revealed that sparse-turn conversations cause projector thrashing. If the conversation pattern continues with high ratio of tool_use turns to substantive turns, extending to 55 cycles will produce a tensor with many 0.00-confidence cycles and strand wipeouts—not useful data. REVISED PHASE 1 SCOPE: (1) DOCUMENT THRASHING PATTERN: Current 10-turn tensor demonstrates signal threshold issue. Do not discard; analyze it as evidence of projector behavior under sparse signal. (2) DESIGN TURN BUFFERING: Before extending to 55, redesign run_projection.py to buffer sparse turns. Implement heuristic: if turn char count <100 AND prior turn also <100, do not invoke projector; accumulate into buffer. When buffer char count >500 OR next turn is substantive (>200 chars), flush buffer + next turn as single projection cycle. (3) RE-EXECUTE PHASE 1 WITH BUFFERING: Apply buffered architecture to same 10-turn conversation. Expected: Fewer projection cycles (maybe 5-7 instead of 10), but each cycle has higher signal density and should maintain strand coherence. (4) COMPARE TENSORS: Original 10-cycle tensor vs. buffered tensor will show whether buffering prevents thrashing. (5) IF BUFFERING SUCCESSFUL: Extend Phase 1 to 55 turns with buffering enabled. (6) IF BUFFERING INSUFFICIENT: Consider alternative: PAUSE PHASE 1; redesign Haiku prompt to handle sparse turns gracefully (e.g., instruct Haiku to mark cycles as 'buffering' state, do not generate strands until threshold met). IMMEDIATE ACTION (Cycle 54-55): Implement turn buffering in run_projection.py. Target: Have buffered version ready before end of Cycle 55. This unblocks Phase 1 extension decision.

key_claims.text: Phase 1 plan to extend to 55 turns is INFEASIBLE without architecture change; sparse turns cause thrashing.
Current 10-turn tensor is valuable as evidence of sparse-signal behavior; should be documented, not discarded.
Turn buffering heuristic: if turn <100 chars AND prior turn <100 chars, accumulate; flush when buffer >500 chars OR next turn substantive.
Re-executing Phase 1 with buffering expected to produce 5-7 cycles (vs. 10) with higher signal density and maintained strand coherence.
Comparison of original vs. buffered tensor will validate whether buffering prevents thrashing; success gates Phase 1 extension to 55.

strands.title: REPLAY MODULE PARALLEL TRACK — UNBLOCKED, PROXY.PY REVIEW CRITICAL (CYCLE 54)
strands.content: CYCLE 54 INTEGRATION: Replay module work remains unblocked and orthogonal to Phase 1 projector issues. Cycle 53 instruction was to conduct proxy.py code review in parallel to Phase 1 extension. That task IS STILL CRITICAL and should proceed immediately. The sparse-turn thrashing in Phase 1 is a PROJECTOR SYSTEM issue, not a replay module design issue. PROXY.PY REVIEW TASK: Cycle 53 outlined steps: (1) Extract JSONL schema, field names, data types, nesting. (2) Identify logging patterns: what is logged per turn? (3) Map integration with pager/eval. (4) Document page fault triggers, state management. STATUS: Review has NOT YET OCCURRED (noted in Cycle 53 instructions as 'queued'). This is Cycle 54's opportunity to execute. DELIVERABLE: A one-page summary document: - JSONL field schema (with types and nesting) - Fields logged per turn (turn ID, timestamp, content, metadata) - Pager integration points (where pager reads proxy logs, modifies state) - Eval integration (how eval consumes proxy logs) - Page fault definition (what triggers a page fault in pager logic) UNBLOCKING REPLAY DESIGN: proxy.py review is THE CRITICAL PATH LOCK for replay.py specification. Until proxy.py is understood empirically, replay.py parser design is speculative. By end of Cycle 54, proxy.py review should be COMPLETE, enabling Cycle 55 to proceed with replay.py design (parser spec, reconstruction algorithm, data structures).

key_claims.text: Replay module work is orthogonal to Phase 1 projector issues; review remains critical path and should proceed immediately.
Proxy.py code review has NOT YET OCCURRED; is queued task from Cycle 53, now Cycle 54's priority.
Deliverable: one-page summary with JSONL schema, per-turn fields, pager/eval integration, page fault definition.
Proxy.py review is critical path lock for replay.py design; until understood, replay parser spec remains speculative.
By end of Cycle 54, proxy.py review should be COMPLETE, unblocking Cycle 55 replay.py design (parser spec, reconstruction algorithm).

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed analysis of Haiku prompt quality and why it fails under sparse signal; whether prompt needs refinement or system architecture change.
declared_losses.why: Focus shifted to signal threshold hypothesis; detailed prompt analysis deferred to Phase 2 or future diagnostic cycle. Prompt quality is entangled with architecture (sparse-turn problem may be unsolvable by prompt alone).

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Specific turn-by-turn content of the 10-turn conversation (what user asked, what assistant answered, tool outputs). Only aggregate metrics retained.
declared_losses.why: Token budget and projection scope focus on trajectory patterns and strand stability, not content archaeology. Full conversation can be recovered from source JSONL if needed.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Quantitative turn buffering algorithm (exact thresholds, edge cases, handling of multi-tool turns). Only high-level heuristic specified.
declared_losses.why: Implementation details of buffering require code design cycle; only architectural principle declared at tensor level. Buffering implementation deferred to run_projection.py refactor (Cycle 55).

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Analysis of whether Haiku's T/I/F=0.00 cycles are features (honest signal of unresolvable uncertainty) or bugs (parser/prompt failure). Only diagnosis provided, not evaluation.
declared_losses.why: Honest T/I/F=0.00 may be correct behavior when projector has no semantic grip; cannot distinguish correctness from failure without additional testing. Deferred to Phase 2 (full-session analysis).

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed comparison between Cycle 53's expected trajectory (truth 0.85-0.88 at turn 20) vs. actual trajectory (thrashing, multiple 0.00 cycles). Why did execution diverge?
declared_losses.why: Cycle 53 expectations were based on cycles 1-4 (which were healthy); cycles 5-10 are genuinely different (sparse turns). This is not a prediction failure; it's conditional prediction (if input quality is high). Noted for Phase 2 conditional analysis.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Strand content details from cycles 1-5 (replay module architecture, proxy.py requirements, pre-reading phase). Only aggregate pattern of wipeout/loss retained.
declared_losses.why: Focus shifted to stability pattern analysis; detailed strand content compression loses specificity about replay module state. Recoverable from tensor_cycle_*.json files if replay design needs to reference prior strands.

open_questions: Is the sparse-turn signal threshold a FUNDAMENTAL LIMITATION of Haiku (cannot generate confident claims from 16-char tool signals), or a PROMPT DESIGN ISSUE (Haiku not instructed to handle minimal content)?
Will turn buffering (accumulate <100 char turns, flush at >500 char threshold) eliminate thrashing, or do root causes run deeper (e.g., Haiku cannot integrate across 3-4 buffered turns either)?
Should Phase 1 extension proceed at all, or should Phase 2 planning (full-session analysis) take priority? Is collecting 55 cycles of thrashing data useful, or does it just add noise?
Strand wipeout cycles (6, 8): Are the 0-strand tensors corrupted by the parser, or is Haiku actually returning empty strands array (and we should trust it)?
Can the projector maintain domain-level coherence (replay module thread) across sparse-turn gaps, or will it always reset to meta-level (epistemological reasoning about tensor mechanics)?
Proxy.py review task: Will reading proxy.py and pager.py code provide the signal needed to design replay.py, or is the code too tightly coupled to be understood in isolation?
Turn buffering design: What is the optimal threshold for 'sparse' (current hypothesis <100 chars), and how should buffering interact with timestamps or turn type (user vs. assistant)?
Should Haiku prompt be changed to explicitly instruct: 'If turn is tool_use or procedural, output special state (e.g., 'buffering' mode) instead of attempting to generate strands'?
Phase 1 extended to 55 turns: If executed with buffering, how many projection cycles will result? Current 10 turns → 10 cycles; with buffering, estimate 5-7 cycles for same 55 turns?
Cycles 9-10 meta-process stability: Is this a REAL recovery (projector learning to maintain state), or COINCIDENTAL (meta-reasoning happens to be stable because it's self-referential)?
After proxy.py review complete (Cycle 54), what is the minimum viable scope for replay.py implementation? Full parser + reconstructor, or MVP proof-of-concept first?
Should we version-control the raw tensor JSON files, or only the analysis/metrics? Current 10-turn experiment produced ~10 JSON files; tracking all for 55 turns?

[
  "CYCLE 54-55 IMMEDIATE: TURN BUFFERING IMPLEMENTATION & PROXY.PY REVIEW (PARALLEL TRACKS)",
  "",
  "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
  "",
  "TRACK A: TURN BUFFERING IMPLEMENTATION (run_projection.py refactor)",
  "",
  "GOAL: Redesign run_projection.py to buffer sparse turns, eliminate thrashing.",
  "",
  "CURRENT BEHAVIOR (cycles 1-10 analysis):",
  "  - Every turn projects individually, even if turn is 16-char tool_use signal",
  "  - Result: cycles 6, 8 return 0 strands, T=0.00 (projector has no semantic grip)",
  "  - Cycles 5-8 show oscillating wipeout pattern (strand count: 7→0→3→0→4→3)",
  "",
  "BUFFERING HEURISTIC (high-level design):",
  "  - Monitor turn-by-turn char count as JSONL is parsed",
  "  - If turn_i char count <100 AND turn_{i-1} char count <100:",
  "    - Add turn_i to buffer (do NOT invoke projector yet)",
  "    - Increment buffer char count",
  "  - If buffer char count exceeds 500 OR next turn is substantive (>200 chars):",
  "    - Invoke projector on accumulated buffer + next turn as single 'batch turn'",
  "    - Reset buffer, continue",
  "  - If turn is end-of-conversation:",
  "    - Flush remaining buffer (if any) as final projection cycle",
  "",
  "IMPLEMENTATION STEPS:",
  "",
  "  STEP 1: Refactor run_projection.py main loop",
  "    Current structure (pseudocode):",
  "      for turn in turns:",
  "        tensor = project_turn(turn)",
  "        save_tensor(tensor)",
  "",
  "    New structure:",
  "      buffer = []",
  "      buffer_chars = 0",
  "      for turn in turns:",
  "        if should_buffer(turn, buffer):",
  "          buffer.append(turn)",
  "          buffer_chars += len(turn.content)",
  "        else:",
  "          if buffer:",
  "            flush_buffer(buffer + [turn])  # Project accumulated + current",
  "            buffer = []",
  "            buffer_chars = 0",
  "          else:",
  "            tensor = project_turn(turn)  # Project current alone",
  "",
  "  STEP 2: Define should_buffer() predicate",
  "    def should_buffer(turn, buffer):",
  "      if len(turn.content) >= 100:",
  "        return False  # Substantive turn, project immediately",
  "      if not buffer or len(buffer) == 0:",
  "        return True  # First turn in buffer, accumulate",
  "      if len(turn.content) < 50 AND buffer[-1].content < 50:",
  "        return True  # Both sparse, accumulate",
  "      return False  # Mixed sparsity, flush and project",
  "",
  "  STEP 3: Define flush_buffer(turns) method",
  "    Concatenate all turns in buffer + next turn into single 'batch turn' content",
  "    Format: '[turn_1_role]: [turn_1_content]\\n[turn_2_role]: [turn_2_content]\\n...'",
  "    Invoke projector on batch as single cycle",
  "    Save tensor with note: 'buffered_from_turns_[start:end]'",
  "",
  "  STEP 4: Update cycle counter logic",
  "    Current: cycle_num = turn_index in conversation",
  "    New: cycle_num = projection_invocation_count (may be <55 if buffering merges turns)",
  "    Rationale: Each tensor represents one projection cycle, not one turn",
  "",
  "  STEP 5: Test on first 10-turn batch",
  "    Command:",
  "      cd /home/tony/projects/hamutay",
  "      python -m hamutay.run_projection_buffered \\",
  "        /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl \\",
  "        ./experiments/first_projection_buffered/ \\",
  "        10",
  "",
  "    EXPECTED OUTPUT:",
  "      Fewer projection cycles (5-7 instead of 10)",
  "      Each cycle with higher char count (buffered turns combined)",
  "      No T=0.00 cycles (buffer eliminates sparse-signal collapse)",
